events.text: Now the fork. One command will clone the entire repo, the app, the database, the config files, in seconds, regardless of size. One gigabyte, one hundred gigabytes, one terabyte: same time, every time.
Both repos exist side by side now. Same machine, same disk, two completely independent environments.
Let us prove the isolation. In the original, our marker file is right where we left it. The fork existence did not change anything in the parent.
Now let us delete that file, but only inside the fork. We are making a destructive change here.
Back to the original. The marker file is still here, untouched. Same image, separate Docker daemons, separate filesystems.
When you are done, just delete the fork. The original stays exactly as it was. Fork, experiment, break things, delete. No risk.
